sections.paragraph:
m. (from the root זוּן), food, Gen 45:23.

מזון

Ch. id. Dan 4:9.

I. מָזֹור m. (from the root זוּר No. I.), the pressing together, binding up of a wound; hence used figuratively of a remedy applied to the wounds of the state, Hos 5:13 Jer 30:13.

II. מָזֹור (from the root זוּר No. II., 1), m. falsehood; hence, fraud, insidious dealing, Oba 1:7, as it is well rendered by LXX., Vulg., Chald., Syr. Others understand it to mean a net, or snare, from the Aramean מְזַר to spread out.
